fact: October 5 is exactly 278 days into the year, which means there are only 87 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels are frantically burying nuts right now - they can bury up to 10,000 acorns in a single season and forget where half of them are!
The sun is setting about 2 minutes earlier each day around October 5, making the days shrink faster than a deflating balloon.
Maple trees are pumping out their brightest red and orange colors right now because they're basically having a sugar rush - all that stored sugar creates the brilliant colors!
October 5 falls smack in the middle of Libra season, which means the zodiac scales are perfectly balanced - just like how day and night were almost equal length two weeks ago!
Monarch butterflies are flying over 2,000 miles south right now, and the ones migrating have never made this journey before - they're using built-in GPS that scientists still don't fully understand!
The moon around October 5 is waxing, which means it's growing fatter each night like it's been eating too much cosmic cake.
In many places, morning frost starts appearing on grass around this date because the ground loses heat faster than the air - it's like the Earth is getting its first taste of winter's icy breath!
Spiders are at their absolute biggest and scariest right now because they've been eating bugs all summer long and are ready to lay eggs before winter hits.
October 5 is roughly 13 days after the autumn equinox, which means we're officially sliding into the darker half of the year in the Northern Hemisphere.
Geese flying south in their V-formation are honking constantly right now because they're basically having a 1,000-mile-long family road trip argument about directions!
